name: AKTI ERP Phase 5B1 — Platform Experience Substrate Ticket Pack v1

canonical_planning_basis.external_review_verdict: PHASE_5B1_PLAN_APPROVED — READY TO MERGE PR #17 AND CREATE TICKET PACK
locked_corrections: tickets is an array of ticket objects, not an object keyed by ID.
Queue has 27 entries: P5B1-000 through P5B1-025 plus P5B1-GATE.
P5B1-006 confirms/wires logo_url from OrganizationSetting branding assets or stops before P5B1-008/P5B1-009.
P5B1-023 owns route screen contracts and non-route component contract requirements.
P5B1-024 has concrete runtime/contract/doc proof MCR.
P5B1-025 owns closure readiness, Phase 5C handoff, and audit package preparation.

phase_boundary: Phase 5B1 is substrate only. No Phase 5C UI implementation. No Phase 6 business modules.
ticket_quality_rule: Implementation is not stale by itself. Tickets become stale. Apply maximum concrete capability within the approved scope of each ticket. Evidence supports delivery; it does not replace working software. Exact-file planning is required before edits.

tickets.title: Baseline/control and repo-state evidence
tickets.objective: Establish Phase 5B1 execution baseline, source authority, exact queue, branch state, and non-scope boundaries before implementation tickets run.
scope: Record current branch/head, source_main_head, ticket-pack parse result, queue count, ticket count, and source authority map.
Confirm Phase 5B1 is substrate-only and that implementation has not started.
Create ticket artifact evidence only.
non_scope: No Phase 5C implementation.
No Phase 6 business modules.
No production deployment, secrets, real providers, runtime AI, or marketplace behavior.
No unrelated refactors or package/lockfile changes.
required_outputs: Baseline evidence artifact with branch/head/source authority/queue validation/non-scope confirmation.
tests_required: JSON parse and queue/ticket parity check for the Phase 5B1 ticket pack.
stop_conditions: Required source authority is missing or conflicts with Prisma, contracts, manifests, generated registry, ADRs, active docs, tests, or validation evidence.
Implementation would start Phase 5C UI polish, Phase 6 business modules, Golden Module work, marketplace, real providers, runtime AI, deployment, or secrets access.
Implementation would invent capabilities, APIs, roles, modules, permissions, events, screens, or business rules not authorized by the ticket.
Exact-file planning discovers stale paths, overlapping ownership, or required files outside ticket scope without conditional replan authority.
Validation fails after bounded repair inside ticket scope.
acceptance_criteria: Baseline artifact exists and proves the 27-entry queue, 27 ticket objects, and source authority map.

tickets.title: Cross-substrate tenant/security/no-fake-surface validation
tickets.objective: Prove the Phase 5B1 substrate preserves tenant isolation, capability filtering, and no-fake-surface constraints across modules, profile, branding, status, data controls, and screen registry.
scope: Add targeted negative/proof tests across role-aware modules and read APIs.
Add static/doc validation for no fake screen registry surfaces.
Record runtime-proven versus document-proven evidence.
non_scope: No Phase 5C implementation.
No Phase 6 business modules.
No production deployment, secrets, real providers, runtime AI, or marketplace behavior.
No unrelated refactors or package/lockfile changes.

tests_required: Runtime tests for modules/profile/branding/status/data-controls and static validation for screen registry no-fake-surface rules.

stop_conditions: Required source authority is missing or conflicts with Prisma, contracts, manifests, generated registry, ADRs, active docs, tests, or validation evidence.

acceptance_criteria: Unauthorized actor cannot see CRM/module cards without required capability.
Visibility does not equal import/export/delete/admin authority.
Organization profile response is tenant-scoped.
Branding/status/data-control reads do not expose another organization.
No Phase 5C screen contract authorizes fake revenue, fake CRM pipeline data, fake notifications, fake analytics, or future business modules as active surfaces.
Evidence distinguishes runtime-proven checks from contract/doc-proven checks.
